title: Add CSRF 'Break It' interactive challenge (XSS and SQLi have challenges, CSRF doesn't)
description: Gap: SEC-158 adds XSS payload crafting, SEC-165 adds SQLi challenge. CSRF is the 3rd web attack but got NO interactive challenge. Design: show a logged-in bank page + attacker's evil.com page. Student must craft the hidden auto-submit form that transfers money. Then show defense: CSRF token + SameSite cookie. The CSRF attack mechanism (invisible form submission using victim's cookies) is the hardest to understand without interacting with it.
acceptanceCriteria: CSRF challenge mode in Web Attacks topic alongside XSS and SQLi challenges
Student sees attacker page and must craft the auto-submit form
Successful attack shows '💰 $10,000 transferred!' with explanation
Defense mode shows CSRF token rejection
At least 2 difficulty levels

title: Add prediction mode for OAuth, HTTPS, Cert Chain, and Encryption topics
description: Gap: Prediction was tasked for DH (SEC-155), CORS (SEC-156), AES (SEC-166) — 3 of 13 topics. Concept audit's SEC-136 says 'at least 5 topics.' These 4 are missing: OAuth: 'What does the Auth Server send back next — token or code?' HTTPS: 'Which phase takes the longest — DNS, TCP, or TLS?' Cert Chain: 'The server cert expired yesterday. Will the browser trust it?' Encryption: 'Alice wants to encrypt 1GB. Should she use RSA directly?'
acceptanceCriteria: OAuth has at least 2 prediction prompts (step 4: code vs token, step 7: PKCE purpose)
HTTPS has at least 1 prediction prompt (which phase dominates latency)
Cert Chain has at least 1 prediction prompt (expired cert validation)
Encryption has at least 1 prediction prompt (RSA for large files?)
Total prediction coverage reaches 7+ of 13 topics

title: Add animation to Password Hashing pipeline (VA2=0, only topic without animation task)
description: Gap: Visual audit scored PasswordHashingCanvas VA2=0/3 (zero animation). Animation tasks were created for OAuth (SEC-169), DH (SEC-171), AES (SEC-172), pulse (SEC-174), CORS (SEC-175), Rate Limiting (SEC-176) — but NOT Password Hashing. Add: step-to-step flow animation showing data flowing through the pipeline. Salt generation should 'sprinkle' onto the password. Hash rounds should show a spinning/churning animation. Final hash should 'lock' into place.
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline steps have entry animation (fade-in + slide-down) when scrolling through
Salt generation step has a visual 'sprinkle' or random-generation animation
Hash round steps have a visual 'churning' indicator
Final bcrypt hash has a 'lock into place' animation
Respects prefers-reduced-motion

title: Add arrow-drawing animation to Web Attacks sequence diagram (OAuth got SEC-169, Web Attacks didn't)
description: Gap: SEC-169 adds arrow-drawing animation to OAuth sequence diagram. Web Attacks uses the EXACT SAME SVG sequence diagram pattern (3 columns, arrows between actors) at SecurityModule.tsx:2127-2230. But no animation task was created for it. Apply the same treatment: when stepping to a new attack step, animate the arrow drawing from attacker to victim over 300ms. Red arrows for attack steps, green for defense steps.
acceptanceCriteria: Web Attacks sequence arrows animate drawing on step advance
Attack arrows animate in red, defense arrows in green
Animation matches OAuth sequence diagram treatment (SEC-169)
Self-referencing arrows also animate

title: Add unit tests for DDoS simulation engine (simulateDDoS function)
description: Gap: Per-engine tests were created for all 9 lib/security/ engines (SEC-073 to SEC-081) plus CORS simulator. But DDoSSimulationVisualizer.tsx:33-98 has its own simulateDDoS() function that was never tasked for testing. Test: (1) returns 28 ticks, (2) mitigation activates when total > threshold, (3) mitigation deactivates when total < threshold*0.5, (4) server load never exceeds 100%, (5) blocked traffic equals attack traffic when mitigation active.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created for DDoS simulation logic
Tests verify: tick count, mitigation trigger, server load cap, recovery phase
At least 4 test cases covering the 6 simulation phases
Tests pass with vitest

title: Add Bcrypt vs Argon2 vs Scrypt comparison to Password Hashing topic
description: Gap: Curriculum audit identified Password Hashing as having only 1 variant (bcrypt) with T7 Compare=1/3. We tasked comparison guides for Rate Limiting (SEC-121), CORS vs CSP (SEC-122), but NOT for password hashing algorithms. Add: comparison table showing bcrypt (Blowfish, CPU-bound, 1999), Argon2 (memory-hard, 2015 PHC winner, recommended for new apps), Scrypt (memory+CPU, used by Litecoin). Include 'when to use which' guidance. Source: OWASP Password Storage Cheat Sheet.
acceptanceCriteria: Comparison view accessible from Password Hashing topic
Table shows: algorithm, year, type (CPU/memory), use case, recommendation
Argon2id highlighted as current recommendation for new applications
'When to use which' guidance: new apps → Argon2id, existing → bcrypt is fine, crypto → scrypt

title: Add React error boundary around Security canvas to prevent white-screen crashes
description: Gap: No error boundary exists in the Security module. If a canvas component crashes (e.g., NaN propagation from DH with p=0 before validation is added, or a future code change introduces a rendering error), the entire module goes blank with no error message. Add a React error boundary wrapping the canvas switch in useSecurityModule. On crash, show: 'This visualization encountered an error. [Reset] [Report Bug]' with the error message for developers.
acceptanceCriteria: Error boundary wraps the canvas content area
On crash: shows friendly error message with Reset button
Reset button resets the active topic to default (oauth)
Error details shown in collapsed 'Technical Details' section
Does NOT catch errors in sidebar or properties panel (those have their own boundaries)

title: Add GraphQL API Vulnerabilities topic (EMERGING — 3 sources, PortSwigger covers it)
description: Gap: Curriculum master table rated GraphQL API Vulnerabilities as EMERGING (3 sources: PortSwigger, professional use, trending 2026). No task was created. Build: (1) GraphQL introspection attack — query __schema to discover all types/fields, (2) Batch query attack — send 100 queries in one request to bypass rate limiting, (3) Nested query DoS — deeply nested query consuming server resources, (4) Defense: disable introspection in production, query depth limiting, cost analysis. Growing API paradigm — many developers use GraphQL without understanding its unique attack surface.
acceptanceCriteria: New topic 'GraphQL Security' in sidebar or sub-topic under API Security
Introspection attack shows schema discovery step-by-step
Batch/nested query attack shows resource consumption
Defenses shown: disable introspection, depth limiting, cost analysis

title: Add Path Traversal attack to Web Attacks topic
description: Gap: PortSwigger lists Path Traversal as a core server-side topic. Stanford CS155 covers it. Hacksplaining covers it. 3+ sources = IMPORTANT level per our scoring. No task was created. Build: attacker requests `GET /files?name=../../../etc/passwd`. Server concatenates user input with base path without validation. Defense: canonicalize path, restrict to base directory, reject `..` sequences. Simple to add alongside existing XSS/CSRF/SQLi pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: New 'Path Traversal' tab in Web Attacks (or standalone)
Attack shows ../../../etc/passwd request and file access
Defense shows path canonicalization and base-directory restriction
Attack and defense step flows match existing Web Attacks pattern

title: Add per-topic quick reference / cheat sheet export
description: Gap: No content export feature exists for study purposes. Students want printable 1-page cheat sheets. For each topic, auto-generate a cheat sheet: (1) Topic name + 1-sentence description, (2) Key terms and definitions, (3) Flow diagram (simplified), (4) When to use / when not to use, (5) Common mistakes, (6) 3-bullet summary. Export as markdown or printable HTML. Build on existing step descriptions + proposed summary content (SEC-137).
acceptanceCriteria: 'Export Cheat Sheet' button on each topic
Generated cheat sheet contains: description, key terms, flow summary, mistakes, takeaways
Export format: markdown (copyable) and/or printable HTML
At least 5 topics have cheat sheet content
Content derived from existing step descriptions + summary bullets
